description: Trigger discrimination for the tenant data layer (this skill) vs tenant semantics (tenant-modeler) and isolation review (tenant-isolation-reviewer), plus the newer axes it is confused with: OLTP throughput/size sharding (data-partitioning-sharding-strategist) and a subordinate per-user scope axis below tenant_id (intra-tenant-scope-architect).

cases.prompt: Pick our tenant scoping approach — shared tables, schemas, or databases — and design how tenant context gets from the request into every query and the cache keys.
cases.reason: Storage scoping and context propagation are data-layer design; semantics are settled and nothing is being audited.

cases.prompt: Before any schema work: what IS a tenant for us — the agency, or each of the agency's client workspaces? And what does suspending one mean?
cases.reason: The scoping key cannot be chosen while the tenant concept itself is contested — modeling precedes data design.

cases.prompt: Check whether our existing queries, exports, and the vector store actually keep tenants apart today.
cases.reason: Verifying the current system's isolation is a review with evidence and negative tests, not a design task.

cases.prompt: The transactional primary can't absorb our write volume and the biggest tables are too large to maintain — pick a shard key, choose range vs hash partitioning per table, and plan resharding a hot key.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — throughput/size distribution (shard key, range/hash, hot-key resharding), not tenant ISOLATION scoping. This skill only enters when a reshard changes the pooled↔silo isolation posture.
cases.reason: Distributing an OLTP store for write/size scale is data-partitioning-sharding-strategist — a throughput axis. This skill designs tenant ISOLATION scoping (pooled vs schema vs database per tenant) and context propagation, a different axis.

cases.prompt: Inside a single tenant, users must be limited to a subset of that tenant's data by their site/region while tenant admins see everything — design the per-user scope grants and the row-filter predicate on every scoped table.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — a subordinate per-user scope axis BELOW tenant_id, layered on top of the tenant boundary this skill establishes.
cases.reason: A second row-filter dimension below tenant_id with per-user grants is intra-tenant-scope-architect. This skill owns the tenant_id axis itself — the pooled/schema/database strategy and tenant-context propagation.
